message:
fuzz: add canonical-CBOR + manifest-TOML round-trip fuzz targets

Two new libfuzzer targets covering deterministic-output boundaries —
the canonical-CBOR serializer and the TOML manifest parser — where a
silent drift between parse and re-serialize is exactly the kind of bug
that bypasses unit tests but breaks cross-agent mesh agreement.

## fuzz_targets/canonicalize_map_deterministic.rs

Structure-aware target for `fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor`, biased
toward the `canonicalize_map` pipeline (sort / dedup / nested-
canonicalize) rather than spending budget on reaching any CBOR at all.

Generator shape:

* `Map` 50% / `Array` 20% / leaf 30%, capped at depth 6 so recursion
  doesn't exhaust the fuzzer's time budget per input.
* Per-map fan-out ≤ 64, string/bytes ≤ 32B, input ≤ 32 KiB. Shape caps
  keep exec/s high; the canonicalizer's real limits
  (MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH = 128, MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES = 64
  MiB) are already tested by targeted unit tests — this harness wants
  many small inputs, not a handful of giant ones.
* Skips Value::Tag because the canonicalizer deliberately rejects
  tags; exercising the reject path would waste coverage on a
  documented no-op.

Oracle: idempotence of canonical output plus structural determinism —
same logical map with permuted entry order must produce byte-identical
output.

## fuzz_targets/manifest_toml_roundtrip.rs

Parses arbitrary bytes as TOML via ConnectorManifest::parse_str_unchecked
(the narrow TOML-parsing boundary — parse_str additionally runs
validate, which rejects many syntactically-parseable shapes; the
unchecked variant lets the harness exercise the full space of
parseable manifests rather than only the validator-approved subset).

Then re-serializes via toml::to_string, re-parses the result, and
requires structural equality. Caps input at 64 KiB and serialized
output at 1 MiB so a runaway serializer producing arbitrarily large
output is a *correctness* failure the harness will catch rather than
run indefinitely through.

## fuzz/Cargo.toml

* Registers fuzz_canonicalize_map_deterministic and
  fuzz_manifest_toml_roundtrip as new [[bin]] targets.
* Adds toml = "1.0" to the fuzz crate's dependencies so the round-
  trip target can call toml::to_string directly.

## fuzz/Cargo.lock

Catch-up: picks up toml 1.1.2+spec-1.1.0 (for the new TOML target)
and fcp-crypto as a dependency of an fcp-* crate on the already-
applied dependency graph for crates the fuzz crate reaches via its
path deps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -20,6 +20,7 @@ serde = { version = "1.0.228", features = ["derive"] }
 serde_json = "1.0.145"
 sha2 = "0.10"
 tempfile = "3"
+toml = "1.0"
 
 fcp-async-core = { path = "../crates/fcp-async-core" }
 fcp-audit = { path = "../crates/fcp-audit" }
@@ -254,5 +255,19 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_manifest_toml_roundtrip"
+path = "fuzz_targets/manifest_toml_roundtrip.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_canonicalize_map_deterministic"
+path = "fuzz_targets/canonicalize_map_deterministic.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
